data.title: Stryker raises dividend by 4.8% to $0.88
data.description:
• Stryker (SYK) declares $0.88/share quarterly dividend, 4.8% increase from prior dividend of $0.84.
  • Forward yield 0.99%
  • Payable Jan. 30; for shareholders of record Dec. 31; ex-div Dec. 31.
  • See SYK Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: ZenaTech signs offer to acquire surveying firm in US
data.description:
• ZenaTech (ZENA) on Thursday said it would acquire a surveying firm headquartered in the Mountain West/Rocky Mountain region of the US with existing licensing to operate in multiple states.
  • "The potential acquisition enhances ZenaTech’s presence in the region and capacity to provide drone-enabled advanced land surveying as well as drone services for modern crop and livestock management, wildfire management, and land stewardship," the company said.
  • ZENA -0.61% premarket to $3.24.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: AC Immune surges on positive mid-stage trial data for Parkinson’s drug
data.description:
AC Immune (ACIU) added ~30% in the premarket on Thursday after the Swiss biotech announced positive initial results from a mid-stage trial for ACI-7104.056, an immunotherapy targeting early Parkinson’s disease.

Based on interim data from Part 1 of its Phase 2 VacSYn trial, which enrolled 34 patients with early PD, the company said that the placebo-controlled study met all target criteria for immunogenicity.

According to ACIU, all trial subjects included in the analysis had received at least 12 months (48 weeks) of ACI-7104.056, which is designed to act on alpha-synuclein (a-syn), a pathology causing PD.

The experimental therapy was generally safe and well-tolerated, the company said, citing data at weeks 50 and 76.

AC Immune (ACIU) added that the readout demonstrated for the first time that targeting a-syn pathology with active immunotherapy could slow the rate of PD progression.

The company expects to meet with regulatory authorities to obtain feedback regarding its plans to accelerate the drug's development towards registration. Final data from Part 1 of the study are anticipated in mid-2026.

data.title: Diamond Hill stock jumps on ~$473M acquisition by First Eagle
data.description:
Diamond Hill (DHIL) stock was surging after the boutique investment management firm announced an ~$473M acquisition by First Eagle Investments.

Shares were +42.58% Thursday pre-market to $167.50.

Under the terms of the transaction, First Eagle Investments is set to pay $175 per share in cash. The purchase price represents a 49% premium over Diamond Hill’s closing share price of $117.48 on December 10.

The acquisition cost represents a 44% premium over the company's 30-day volume-weighted average price.

The deal is expected to increase First Eagle's footprint in traditional fixed income.

Diamond Hill said it will maintain its headquarters in Columbus and operate under its existing brand. As part of the agreement, the target company will not pay quarterly dividends through closing.

Pro forma for the transaction, First Eagle's assets under management and assets under advisement are expected to be ~$208B as of September 30.

The transaction is expected to close by the third quarter of 2026.

data.title: Alnylam announces partial repurchase of convertible notes due 2027
data.description:
Alnylam Pharmaceuticals (ALNY) said it reached privately negotiated agreements to repurchase for cash about $34.4M of its 1.00% Convertible Senior Notes due 2027.

The company will pay roughly $51.9M in cash, including accrued and unpaid interest.

The final aggregate cash repurchase price is subject to adjustment as a portion of the repurchase price will be based in part on the daily volume-weighted average price per share of the company’s common stock over an agreed measurement period beginning on, and including, December 11, 2025.

The repurchases are expected to close shortly after completion of the measurement period, subject to the satisfaction of customary closing conditions. Following such closings, around $362.8 million principal amount of the notes will remain outstanding.

data.title: Sarepta to refinance $291M of existing notes due 2027
data.description:
• Sarepta Therapeutics (SRPT) has agreed to privately exchange about $291.4M of its existing 1.25% convertible notes due 2027 for an equal amount of new 4.875% convertible notes due 2030, plus approximately $31.6M in cash.
  • The exchange is expected to close on or about December 18, 2025, subject to customary closing conditions. Following the closing, around $158.6 million of the existing convertible notes will remain outstanding with terms unchanged.
  • The new convertible notes will be issued as part of the same series as the ~$602 million of 4.875% convertible senior notes due 2030 originally issued in August 2025.
  • Following the closing of the exchange, the principal amount of the company’s 4.875% convertible senior notes due 2030 will total $893.4 million.
  • SRPT -2.7% premarket on Thursday.

data.title: Oracle slumps, but analysts look past weak results towards AI growth
data.description:
Oracle (ORCL) was in focus on Thursday as Wall Street firms defended the IT giant after it reported mixed results, and said the growth from artificial intelligence will be surprising in the years to come.

Shares fell more than 12% in premarket trading, bringing down other AI-related stocks such as Microsoft (MSFT), Nvidia (NVDA), AMD (AMD) and others.

Wedbush Securities analyst Dan Ives said investors should look past the weaker-than-expected revenue and at the $523B in remaining performance obligations, or RPO, which topped Wall Street's forecast of $500B. 

“Oracle added impressively $69 billion in incremental RPO (vs. $317 billion last quarter including the $300 billion OpenAI deal) and this compares to RPO of $8 billion, $33 billion, and $2 billion in the prior quarters,” Ives wrote in a note to clients. “This is the important number we are laser focused on vs. the actual top-line result this quarter or guidance next quarter. Taking a step back, this should translate into eye-popping revenue growth of 17%+ in FY26 and then roughly 35% in FY27 and ~45% in FY28 as conversion of AI backlog takes place over the coming years.”

For fiscal 2026, Oracle reaffirmed its revenue guidance of $67B, in-line with the $67B estimate. The company also said on its earnings call that the additional remaining performance obligations can be monetized in the future and eventually convert to revenue in short order. Oracle expects roughly $4B in additional revenue in fiscal 2027, management added.

Additionally, the concerns about the debt levels that Oracle will need to raise to fund its massive AI-infused expansion are likely overblown, Ives added. 

“Furthermore, Oracle will likely need to raise less than the $100 billion in debt to service the Capex needed for the New Cloud ARR/RPO and could be significantly under this number when all is said it done in our view,” Ives added. “This is naturally a focus point of investors, and we believe it's a high class problem that Oracle is dealing with on the AI demand front.”

BNP Paribas analyst Stefan Slowinski agreed and said the results, particularly the revenue from Oracle Cloud Infrastructure, were a “relief.” 

“That said, with concerns that there may have been delays in the key Abilene datacenter that would cause a miss, the 66% [growth in constant currency] is somewhat of a relief to us,” Slowinski wrote in a note to clients. “[Free cash flow] fell to -$10bn in FQ2 (driven by $12bn of capex, ~46% ahead of [consensus]) indicating that OCI growth will likely come through strong in H2, and thus no datacenter build delays are visible, which also appears as a relief.”

Slowinski has a Buy rating and $290 price target on Oracle.

data.title: North Shore Uranium announces $3 million private placement
data.description:
• North Shore Uranium (NSU:CA) announced on Thursday a non-brokered private placement offering for gross proceeds of up to $3 million through the issuance of units of the company at $0.25 per unit.
  • Each unit consists of one common share and one-half of one share purchase warrant. Each warrant entitles the holder to purchase one common share at $0.40 per warrant share for a period of two years from closing of the offering.
  • The company said that the net proceeds of the offering will be used for exploration of the Rio Puerco uranium project in New Mexico, continued exploration of its Saskatchewan uranium properties, the costs of the offering and for general working capital.
  • Source: Press Release
